mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 1.0 percentage point signals abrupt labor market deterioration and collapsing consumer spending, which forces equity markets to price in severe earnings revisions and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or out-of-sample directional accuracy falls below 40%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction across consumer and capital expenditure channels, which reduces aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a directional accuracy above 55% in out-of-sample testing, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production reflects immediate manufacturing output contraction and supply chain bottlenecks, which triggers short-term risk-off positioning and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following a decline in INDPRO are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the signal reverses direction during periods of stable monetary policy.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.5 percent while the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, aggressive monetary accommodation offsets labor market weakness, establishing a valuation floor that produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to yield positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered observations, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a pooled cross-sectional regression.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP growth rate falling below negative 1.0 percent confirms a formal economic contraction, which forces institutional investors to de-risk equity portfolios and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following confirmed GDP contractions are positive in > 55% of cases, or the signal's out-of-sample directional accuracy drops below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained monthly reading of the consumer price index below 2.5 percent indicates benign inflation dynamics, which preserves real corporate profit margins and supports higher equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during periods of CPIAUCSL below 2.5 are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the signal's Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll employment decline exceeding 500,000 persons combined with a simultaneous drop in retail sales signals a synchronized collapse in labor income and consumer spending, which directly reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns with directional accuracy above 60% in out-of-sample testing, or the interaction term loses predictive power (p > 0.10) when controlling for monetary policy changes.
